110.code: void BFS(struct ver  G[ ], int v)
110.reason: [Recursive BFS implementation is incorrect and leads to infinite recursion or stack overflow. BFS should be iterative using a queue, not recursive. Also, the logic inside incorrectly processes only one neighbor per call and then recurses on the next queue element without proper loop control.]

124.reason: [This recursive call causes undefined behavior when 'first >= last' (i.e., queue is empty), leading to accessing uninitialized or invalid memory in 'dui'. It also doesn't implement BFS correctly as it lacks a loop to process all nodes level by level.]
